message: feat: add edge tts fallback provider

files.patch: @@ -8,6 +8,7 @@ Docs: https://docs.clawd.bot¥n - Ollama: provider discovery + docs. (#1606) Thanks @abhaymundhara. https://docs.clawd.bot/providers/ollama¥n ¥n ### Changes¥n+- TTS: add Edge TTS provider fallback, defaulting to keyless Edge with MP3 retry on format failures. (#1668) Thanks @steipete. https://docs.clawd.bot/tts¥n - Docs: expand FAQ (migration, scheduling, concurrency, model recommendations, OpenAI subscription auth, Pi sizing, hackable install, docs SSL workaround).¥n - Docs: add verbose installer troubleshooting guidance.¥n - Docs: update Fly.io guide notes.

@@ -8,21 +8,37 @@ read_when:¥n ¥n # Text-to-speech (TTS)¥n ¥n-Clawdbot can convert outbound replies into audio using ElevenLabs or OpenAI.¥n+Clawdbot can convert outbound replies into audio using ElevenLabs, OpenAI, or Edge TTS.¥n It works anywhere Clawdbot can send audio; Telegram gets a round voice-note bubble.¥n ¥n ## Supported services¥n ¥n - **ElevenLabs** (primary or fallback provider)¥n - **OpenAI** (primary or fallback provider; also used for summaries)¥n+- **Edge TTS** (primary or fallback provider; uses `node-edge-tts`, default when no API keys)¥n ¥n-## Required keys¥n+### Edge TTS notes¥n ¥n-At least one of:¥n+Edge TTS uses Microsoft Edge's online neural TTS service via the `node-edge-tts`¥n+library. It's a hosted service (not local), uses Microsoft¥u2019s endpoints, and does¥n+not require an API key. `node-edge-tts` exposes speech configuration options and¥n+output formats, but not all options are supported by the Edge service. ¥ue200cite¥ue202turn2search0¥ue201¥n+¥n+Because Edge TTS is a public web service without a published SLA or quota, treat it¥n+as best-effort. If you need guaranteed limits and support, use OpenAI or ElevenLabs.¥n+Microsoft's Speech REST API documents a 10¥u2011minute audio limit per request; Edge TTS¥n+does not publish limits, so assume similar or lower limits. ¥ue200cite¥ue202turn0search3¥ue201¥n+¥n+## Optional keys¥n+¥n+If you want OpenAI or ElevenLabs:¥n - `ELEVENLABS_API_KEY` (or `XI_API_KEY`)¥n - `OPENAI_API_KEY`¥n ¥n-If both are configured, the selected provider is used first and the other is a fallback.¥n+Edge TTS does **not** require an API key. If no API keys are found, Clawdbot defaults¥n+to Edge TTS (unless disabled via `messages.tts.edge.enabled=false`).¥n+¥n+If multiple providers are configured, the selected provider is used first and the others are fallback options.¥n Auto-summary uses the configured `summaryModel` (or `agents.defaults.model.primary`),¥n so that provider must also be authenticated if you enable summaries.¥n ¥n@@ -32,12 +48,17 @@ so that provider must also be authenticated if you enable summaries.¥n - [OpenAI Audio API reference](https://platform.openai.com/docs/api-reference/audio)¥n - [ElevenLabs Text to Speech](https://elevenlabs.io/docs/api-reference/text-to-speech)¥n - [ElevenLabs Authentication](https://elevenlabs.io/docs/api-reference/authentication)¥n+- [node-edge-tts](https://github.com/SchneeHertz/node-edge-tts)¥n+- [Microsoft Speech output formats](https://learn.microsoft.com/azure/ai-services/speech-service/rest-text-to-speech#audio-outputs)¥n ¥n ## Is it enabled by default?¥n ¥n No. TTS is **disabled** by default. Enable it in config or with `/tts on`,¥n which writes a local preference override.¥n ¥n+Edge TTS **is** enabled by default once TTS is on, and is used automatically¥n+when no OpenAI or ElevenLabs API keys are available.¥n+¥n ## Config¥n ¥n TTS config lives under `messages.tts` in `clawdbot.json`.¥n@@ -94,6 +115,41 @@ Full schema is in [Gateway configuration](/gateway/configuration).¥n }¥n ```¥n ¥n+### Edge TTS primary (no API key)¥n+¥n+```json5¥n+{¥n+  messages: {¥n+    tts: {¥n+      enabled: true,¥n+      provider: ¥

¥n+      }¥n+    }¥n+  }¥n+}¥n+```¥n+¥n+### Disable Edge TTS¥n+¥n+```json5¥n+{¥n+  messages: {¥n+    tts: {¥n+      edge: {¥n+        enabled: false¥n+      }¥n+    }¥n+  }¥n+}¥n+```¥n+¥n ### Custom limits + prefs path¥n ¥n ```json5¥n@@ -131,7 +187,9 @@ Then run:¥n ¥n - `enabled`: master toggle (default `false`; local prefs can override).¥n - `mode`: `¥

` (fallback is automatic).¥n+- If `provider` is **unset**, Clawdbot prefers `openai` (if key), then `elevenlabs` (if key),¥n+  otherwise `edge`.¥n - `summaryModel`: optional cheap model for auto-summary; defaults to `agents.defaults.model.primary`.¥n   - Accepts `provider/model` or a configured model alias.¥n - `modelOverrides`: allow the model to emit TTS directives (on by default).¥n@@ -147,6 +205,15 @@ Then run:¥n - `elevenlabs.applyTextNormalization`: `auto|on|off`¥n - `elevenlabs.languageCode`: 2-letter ISO 639-1 (e.g. `en`, `de`)¥n - `elevenlabs.seed`: integer `0..4294967295` (best-effort determinism)¥n+- `edge.enabled`: allow Edge TTS usage (default `true`; no API key).¥n+- `edge.voice`: Edge neural voice name (e.g. `en-US-MichelleNeural`).¥n+- `edge.lang`: language code (e.g. `en-US`).¥n+- `edge.outputFormat`: Edge output format (e.g. `audio-24khz-48kbitrate-mono-mp3`).¥n+  - See Microsoft Speech output formats for valid values; not all formats are supported by Edge.¥n+- `edge.rate` / `edge.pitch` / `edge.volume`: percent strings (e.g. `+10%`, `-5%`).¥n+- `edge.saveSubtitles`: write JSON subtitles alongside the audio file.¥n+- `edge.proxy`: proxy URL for Edge TTS requests.¥n+- `edge.timeoutMs`: request timeout override (ms).¥n ¥n ## Model-driven overrides (default on)¥n ¥n@@ -167,7 +234,7 @@ Here you go.¥n ```¥n ¥n Available directive keys (when enabled):¥n-- `provider` (`openai` | `elevenlabs`)¥n+- `provider` (`openai` | `elevenlabs` | `edge`)¥n - `voice` (OpenAI voice) or `voiceId` (ElevenLabs)¥n - `model` (OpenAI TTS model or ElevenLabs model id)¥n - `stability`, `similarityBoost`, `style`, `speed`, `useSpeakerBoost`¥n@@ -225,8 +292,15 @@ These override `messages.tts.*` for that host.¥n   - 48kHz / 64kbps is a good voice-note tradeoff and required for the round bubble.¥n - **Other channels**: MP3 (`mp3_44100_128` from ElevenLabs, `mp3` from OpenAI).¥n   - 44.1kHz / 128kbps is the default balance for speech clarity.¥n-¥n-This is not configurable; Telegram expects Opus for voice-note UX.¥n+- **Edge TTS**: uses `edge.outputFormat` (default `audio-24khz-48kbitrate-mono-mp3`).¥n+  - `node-edge-tts` accepts an `outputFormat`, but not all formats are available¥n+    from the Edge service. ¥ue200cite¥ue202turn2search0¥ue201¥n+  - Output format values follow Microsoft Speech output formats (including Ogg/WebM Opus). ¥ue200cite¥ue202turn1search0¥ue201¥n+  - Telegram `sendVoice` accepts OGG/MP3/M4A; use OpenAI/ElevenLabs if you need¥n+    guaranteed Opus voice notes. ¥ue200cite¥ue202turn1search1¥ue201¥n+  - If the configured Edge output format fails, Clawdbot retries with MP3.¥n+¥n+OpenAI/ElevenLabs formats are fixed; Telegram expects Opus for voice-note UX.¥n ¥n ## Auto-TTS behavior¥n
